records.ety: [OE. nompere, nounpere (also impier, fr. F. impair uneven), fr. OF. nomper uneven, F. non-pair; hence the meaning, uneven, i. e., third person; non not + OF. per even, equal, peer, F. pair; cf. L. impar uneven, unequal. See Non-, and Peer, n.]

defs: A person to whose sole decision a controversy or question between parties is referred; especially, one chosen to see that the rules of a game, as cricket, baseball, or the like, are strictly observed.
quotes: A man, in questions of this kind, is able to be a skillful umpire between himself and others.

defs: A third person, who is to decide a controversy or question submitted to arbitrators in case of their disagreement.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Umpired (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Umpiring.]

defs: To decide as umpire; to arbitrate; to settle, as a dispute.
quotes: Judges appointed to umpire the matter in contest between them, and to decide where the right lies.

defs: To perform the duties of umpire in or for; as, to umpire a game.
To act as umpire or arbitrator.
